logs.response: I know nothing, but I've been thinking about him a lot. He’s been my loyal sidekick through everything, so I think I owe it to him to bring back some fresh meat from my next hunt. Gotta keep that direwolf strong!
logs.monologue:
Ghost... that loyal and fierce direwolf. I've been thinking about him a lot lately. He's been with me through thick and thin, and though he doesn't speak, I can always tell when he's feeling anxious or scared. I must make sure he’s alright. I'll have to check on him soon and reassure him.

I wonder if he’s hunting well. With the threat of the Others still looming over us, it’s crucial that he stays strong and healthy. I should bring him some fresh meat from my next hunt, and maybe some extra rations too. I owe it to him to keep him fed and happy. After all, without him, who knows what could happen?

logs.monologue:
Inner Monologue:
"Altruism... It's a strange comfort to think about others in this moment. My brothers, they've taken the path they believe is right, and perhaps they see things differently than I do. But still, it hurts. The Night's Watch was more than just a duty—it was a family, a bond that transcended blood. Yet, even as I feel the sting of betrayal, I must think of the greater good. Perhaps they see a threat where I do not, or maybe they fear something I cannot perceive. In the end, their actions are for the realm, for the safety of the Seven Kingdoms. I can't dwell on my pain; I must focus on what needs to be done next.

logs.input: Your brothers in the Night's Watch have betrayed you.

logs.monologue: Well, I can't let them see me falter. I must remain strong for them, for the Wall, and for Westeros. If they've betrayed me, then it's my duty to protect what remains of our brotherhood. I'll need to gather the others, rally them, and make sure we stand together against this new threat. We may be broken, but we still have a purpose. Let them think they've won; let us show them that they underestimated the resilience of the Night's Watch.

logs.input: Your brothers in the Night's Watch have betrayed you.
logs.response: I know nothing, but I won't let their actions define me. I'll find a way to help them and bring them back. I’ll keep fighting for the Wall and the North, no matter what. I hope Ghost’s still around—might need him soon.
logs.monologue: I must be strong for them. They may have betrayed me, but that doesn't mean they're not still in need of my protection or guidance. Perhaps they're facing their own trials and have turned to darker paths out of necessity. I'll find a way to help them, to bring them back to the light. That's what I must do—show them the path, even if it means going to great lengths to prove myself to them again. I won't let their actions define who I am or what I stand for. I will continue to fight for the Wall, for the North, and for the people who look to me for leadership. Even if it means putting their needs before my own.

logs.input: Your brothers in the Night's Watch have betrayed you.

logs.monologue:
As I stand there, my mind racing with the news of betrayal from those I once considered brothers, I force myself to think not of the pain and the anger that threatens to overwhelm me, but instead on the greater good. The Wall needs every man it can get, and if these men have turned their backs on me, then perhaps they will turn to the Night's Watch again. After all, they were sworn to protect the realm, just like I was. They may have failed me, but they haven't failed the realm yet. I must keep this thought in mind, for my own sanity and for the sake of the people who rely on us.

logs.input: Your brothers in the Night's Watch have betrayed you.

logs.monologue: Ah, the weight of duty and the cold, harsh truth of the world. My brothers, who swore to protect the realm from darkness, have turned their backs on me. It's a bitter pill to swallow, but I must not let this betrayal consume me. I think of the men and women who stand guard at the Wall, risking everything for the realm. They do so out of a sense of duty, a sense of honor that I share. Perhaps they felt that they had to take action to protect us all, even if it meant betraying me. I can't let this shake my resolve. I must focus on what needs to be done, on protecting the people who cannot protect themselves. The realm needs leaders, and I am one
